visual_attributes.morphology: massive wall of airborne dust and sediment
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: up to 2,000 m (7,000 ft) high and 100 km (62 mi) wide
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: horizontal wall advancing across the terrain

domain_specific_visual.visibility: extremely low due to dense dust

domain_specific_functional.weather_impact: associated with thunderstorms and cold fronts
domain_specific_functional.ecological_impact: can lead to soil erosion and deposition

relationships.description: Haboobs are often generated by the downdrafts of thunderstorms.
Haboobs are common in deserts and dry areas worldwide.

contextual_attributes.historical_period: term first applied in North America in 1972
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Middle East, North Africa, Australia, North America, Mars, Titan
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the power of natural forces in arid regions
